key_suppliers: Third-party service providers for customer support and booking fulfillment
dependencies: Dependence on relationships with travel suppliers (hotels, airlines, car rental companies)
Dependence on third-party technology providers including AWS for cloud infrastructure
Dependence on Global Distribution Systems (GDS) partners for air business
Dependence on payment processors for credit/debit card transactions
Reliance on third-party service providers for customer-facing and internal services
risks: Travel suppliers may reduce compensation, limit access to inventory, or restrict competitive rates
Supplier consolidation (airline and hotel chain mergers) may reduce negotiating power
Suppliers increasingly driving direct bookings through their own websites with better rates and loyalty programs
Payment processors may impose holdbacks or suspend services upon material adverse changes
Payment card networks may increase interchange fees
AWS infrastructure failures could adversely affect platform availability
Third-party service provider failures could disrupt operations
Counterparty risk with financial institutions holding deposits and processing payments
Fraud risks from suppliers and travelers
Credit risk with B2B and Vrbo partners

regulatory_risks: Alternative accommodation regulations: licensing requirements, property registration, data provision, short-term rental restrictions
Travel industry regulations: seller of travel registration, disclosure requirements, state restitution funds
U.S. Department of Transportation regulations for air transportation (advertising, accessibility)
European Union Package Travel Directive imposing liability for package travel performance
Digital Services Act (EU) imposing information gathering and reporting requirements
DAC7 (EU) requiring supplier-related information display and reporting
Data protection and privacy laws: GDPR (EU), CCPA/CPRA (California), 19 other U.S. states with similar laws
Consumer protection laws and regulations globally
Competition and antitrust laws (investigations by National Competition Authorities in Europe and other jurisdictions)
Economic and trade sanction programs (OFAC, DOJ, SEC enforcement)
Tax regulations: occupancy taxes, VAT, sales taxes, digital services taxes, transient taxes
Payment services regulations including enhanced authentication (SCA) and PCI DSS compliance
Pricing display requirements (FTC, California, Minnesota, Congress considering all-in pricing)
City center access restrictions and accommodation supply limitations
Climate-related disclosure requirements (emerging SEC and other regulations)
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax (15% rate) enacted in certain jurisdictions
AI regulation (proposed legislation in jurisdictions where company operates)

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions and trade regulations enforcement by OFAC, DOJ, and foreign regulatory authorities
Compliance with evolving economic sanctions programs
Potential violations leading to monetary damages, civil and criminal penalties
Geopolitical conflicts (war in Ukraine, Israel-Hamas war) affecting travel demand and operations
